name: Late Archaic
description: A prehistoric cultural period in Virginia characterized by distinct archaeological patterns that varied across different physical regions of the Commonwealth.

name: Virginia
description: The state being discussed in terms of its diverse physical regions and Late Archaic archaeological patterns.

name: Coastal Plain
description: A physical region of Virginia that becomes progressively wider moving south from the Potomac, reaching nearly 90 miles east to west along the North Carolina border.

name: Eastern Shore
description: A geographic area that stands as an obvious outlier in the Coastal Plain subdivision scheme.

name: Potomac
description: A major river system that forms the northern boundary of Virginia's Coastal Plain and has long been recognized as having close prehistoric cultural connections to the Northeast.

name: North Carolina
description: The southern border state where Virginia's Coastal Plain reaches its greatest width of nearly 90 miles east to west.

name: Fredericksburg
description: A city north of which the Coastal Plain is essentially limited to a narrow strip between the tidal Potomac shore and the Fall Line.

name: Fall Line
description: A geographic boundary roughly delineated by the Interstate 95 right of way that forms the western edge of the narrow Coastal Plain strip north of Fredericksburg.

name: Interstate 95
description: A modern highway whose right of way roughly delineates the Fall Line in northern Virginia.

name: Piedmont
description: A very large physical region of Virginia that can effectively be split into Northern and Southern sections, with the James River providing a logical demarcation.

name: James River
description: A major river system that provides a logical demarcation between Northern and Southern sections of the Piedmont and defines the middle reaches of the Ridge and Valley region.

name: Blue Ridge
description: A physical region of Virginia that is similarly divided like the Piedmont for archaeological analysis purposes.

name: Ridge and Valley
description: The most expansive physical region of the Commonwealth, covering an area from the very northern tip of Virginia, south and west to Cumberland Gap, Tennessee.

name: Cumberland Gap
description: A geographic landmark that marks the southern and western extent of Virginia's Ridge and Valley region.

name: Tennessee
description: A neighboring state whose river basin may share more cultural similarities with Southwest Virginia than the Middle Atlantic region.

name: Southwest Virginia
description: A distinct cultural region that, while technically part of the greater Ridge and Valley, may share more in common with the Tennessee River basin than the Middle Atlantic.

name: Middle Atlantic
description: A cultural region that Southwest Virginia may have less in common with compared to the Tennessee River basin.

name: Northeast
description: A region that has long been recognized as having close prehistoric cultural connections to the Potomac drainage area.

name: Appalachian Plateau
description: A physical region in Virginia with rugged landscape that falls mostly in Buchanan County, Dickerson County, and portions of Scott County, characterized by a near total lack of recorded Late Archaic period sites.

name: Buchanan County
description: A Virginia county that lies entirely within the Appalachian Plateau province and essentially lacks known Late Archaic site components.

name: Dickerson County
description: A Virginia county that lies entirely within the Appalachian Plateau province and essentially lacks known Late Archaic site components.

name: Scott County
description: A Virginia county with portions falling within the Appalachian Plateau region.

name: Coastal Plain
description: A physical region of the Commonwealth of Virginia, one of the major physiographic provinces.

name: Potomac River
description: A river system forming a drainage basin in the northern region, mentioned in context of the northern Coastal Plain environs.

name: Middle Peninsula
description: A subregion within the Coastal Plain, associated with Rappahannock/York drainage.

name: Rappahannock
description: A river forming part of the drainage system for the Middle Peninsula region.

name: York
description: A river forming part of the drainage system for the Middle Peninsula region.

name: James River
description: A major river in Virginia with its own drainage system, mentioned as both a Coastal Plain and Piedmont drainage region.

name: Carolina Sounds
description: A drainage region in southeastern Virginia, part of the Coastal Plain with distinct material culture and settlement patterns.

name: Dismal Swamp
description: A geographic feature that is part of the Carolina Sounds/Dismal Swamp/Atlantic drainages region.

name: Eastern Shore
description: A subregion within the Coastal Plain physical region of Virginia.

name: Piedmont
description: A major physical region of the Commonwealth, subdivided into Northern, James River, and Southern sections.

name: Northern Piedmont
description: The northern subdivision of the Piedmont physical region.

name: Southern Piedmont
description: The southern subdivision of the Piedmont physical region.

name: Blue Ridge
description: A major physical region of Virginia, divided into Northern and Southern sections.

name: Northern Blue Ridge
description: The northern subdivision of the Blue Ridge physical region.

name: Southern Blue Ridge
description: The southern subdivision of the Blue Ridge physical region.

name: Ridge and Valley
description: A major physical region of Virginia with multiple drainage-based subdivisions including Northern, Central, and Southwest sections.

name: Northern Ridge and Valley
description: The northern subdivision of the Ridge and Valley region, associated with Potomac/Shenandoah drainage.

name: Shenandoah
description: A river forming part of the drainage system for the Northern Ridge and Valley region along with the Potomac.

name: Central Ridge and Valley
description: The central subdivision of the Ridge and Valley region, associated with James River drainage.

name: Southwest Virginia
description: A subdivision of the Ridge and Valley region characterized by New River and Tennessee River headwaters drainage.

name: New River
description: A river system whose headwaters are found in Southwest Virginia within the Ridge and Valley region.

name: Tennessee River
description: A river system whose headwaters are found in Southwest Virginia within the Ridge and Valley region.

name: Appalachian Plateau
description: A major physical region of Virginia, described as undifferentiated in terms of subdivisions.

name: Commonwealth
description: Reference to the Commonwealth of Virginia as a whole, mentioned as the geographic scope for addressing Late Archaic research themes.

name: Late Archaic
description: A cultural period referenced in the context of archaeological research themes across Virginia's physical regions.

name: Savannah River
description: A projectile point and tool tradition that marks the beginning of the Late Archaic cultural/temporal subdivision in Virginia and is dominant throughout the Commonwealth.

name: Late Archaic
description: A cultural and temporal period in Virginia archaeology characterized by multiple hafted tool traditions and the use of steatite vessels.

name: Broadspear complexes
aliases: other Broadspear complexes of the Northeast
description: Tool and projectile point complexes characteristic of the Northeast region during the Late Archaic period.

name: steatite vessels
description: Manufactured stone containers quarried from soapstone and distributed statewide and beyond during the Late Archaic period.

name: Early Woodland
description: A cultural period mentioned in relation to Late Archaic research in Virginia.

name: COVA
description: A publication volume that contains research on Late Archaic and Early Woodland periods in Virginia.

name: McLearen
description: An archaeologist who provided a material culture overview for the Late Archaic period in Virginia in 1991.

name: ground stone grooved ax
description: A groundstone tool type that first appears during the Late Archaic period.

name: Commonwealth
description: Reference to Virginia, where the Savannah River tradition is dominant throughout the region.

name: Coastal Plain
description: A geographic region in Virginia where Savannah River traditions are perhaps most prevalent.

name: Lower James River
description: A geographic area where Savannah River components are particularly common and local quartzite use is ubiquitous.

name: Perkiomen
description: A projectile point type that occurs within the Lower James River environs, though somewhat rare, and is known in greater numbers in other Virginia locations.

name: Susquehanna
description: A projectile point type that occurs within the Lower James River environs along with Perkiomen types.

name: Carolina Sounds
description: A drainage area where a Perkiomen point was recovered at Cactus Hill.

name: Cactus Hill
description: An archaeological site where a Perkiomen point was recovered in the Carolina Sounds drainage.

name: McAvoy
description: Archaeologist who reported the Perkiomen point recovery at Cactus Hill in 1997.

name: Dismal Swamp
description: A geographic area where far greater numbers of Perkiomen points are known.

name: McCary
description: Researcher who documented Perkiomen points from the western Dismal Swamp margins in 1972.

name: Blanton
description: Researcher who documented Perkiomen points from the western Dismal Swamp margins in 2003.

name: Painter
description: Researcher who documented Perkiomen points from extreme southeast Virginia in 1988.

name: Gleach
description: Researcher who documented Perkiomen and Susquehanna types in the Lower James River environs in 1987.

name: Mouer
description: Researcher who documented Perkiomen and Susquehanna types in the Lower James River environs in 1984.

name: Rappahannock drainage
description: A river drainage system mentioned in the context of Late Archaic archaeological investigations.

name: Middle Peninsula
description: A geographic region in Virginia with limited Late Archaic archaeological investigation.

name: Lower Potomac River
description: A river region that provides the best temporal definition of the Late Archaic period through archaeological work.

name: Plum Nelly Site
description: An archaeological site in Northumberland County that yielded Savannah River contexts with radiocarbon dates.

name: Northumberland County
description: A county in Virginia where the Plum Nelly Site is located.

name: Savannah River
description: A projectile point type and cultural tradition dominant in the Late Archaic period, with radiocarbon dates ranging from about 4105 to 3260 BP.

name: Slade site
description: An archaeological site that yielded a Savannah River radiocarbon date of 4070+/-80 BP.

name: Coastal Plain
description: A physiographic region in Virginia with characteristic sandy, acidic soils and fire-cracked rock features.

name: Pony Pasture
description: An archaeological site along the Chickahominy River that yielded a later Savannah River date of 3260 +/-90 BP.

name: Chickahominy River
description: A river in Virginia where the Pony Pasture site is located.

name: Cattle Run point
description: A quartzite projectile point type found fractured and fire-reddened in association with Savannah River variants.

name: Late Archaic
description: An archaeological period characterized by Savannah River point traditions, dating roughly 4100-3200 BP based on radiocarbon evidence.

name: Middle Archaic
description: An earlier archaeological period referenced for comparison of lithic tool assemblages with the Late Archaic.

name: Piedmont
description: A physiographic region in Virginia dominated by the Savannah River tradition and characterized by steatite procurement.

name: James River drainage
description: A river system in Virginia where the Savannah River tradition is dominant and Late Archaic sites are found.

name: Northern Virginia
description: A region in Virginia where steatite quarry sites occur along the western Piedmont.

name: Grayson County
description: A county in Virginia marking the southern extent of steatite quarry sites along the western Piedmont.

name: Charlottesville
description: A city in Virginia that marks one end of the concentrated line of steatite quarry sites.

name: Lynchburg
description: A city in Virginia that marks the other end of the concentrated line of steatite quarry sites.

name: Piedmont
description: Physiographic region of Virginia where relatively little Late Archaic site excavation has been undertaken, except for James River floodplain locations.

name: James River
description: Major river in Virginia with floodplain locations that have been the focus of Late Archaic site excavation in the Piedmont region, including headwaters where Savannah River tradition is prevalent.

name: Blue Ridge
description: Mountain range in Virginia where archaeological work has provided views of Late Archaic material culture, with documented Savannah River components at high elevations.

name: Ridge and Valley
description: Physiographic province in Virginia where Late Archaic material culture has been documented, including Savannah River and Perkiomen components.

name: Shenandoah Valley
description: Valley region in Virginia where Perkiomen components are clustered in the northern area.

name: Shenandoah Mountains
description: Part of the Northern Virginia Blue Ridge where Savannah River components are well documented.

name: Southwest Virginia
description: Region of Virginia where both Savannah River and Tennessee Valley Archaic point types are found, including rare Perkiomen and Susquehanna points.

name: Northern Virginia
description: Region of Virginia that includes the Blue Ridge area where Savannah River components are documented.

name: Tennessee Valley
description: Valley region associated with Archaic point types found in Southwest Virginia.

name: Lower Tennessee Valley
description: Geographic region referenced in relation to FCR filled pit features reported by Chapman.

name: Coastal Plain
description: Physiographic region where observations about Savannah River point size reduction over time have been made.

name: Commonwealth
description: Reference to Virginia, where archaeological survey work has been undertaken across mountainous west and west-central areas.

name: Late Archaic
description: Cultural period characterized by specific material culture including Savannah River and Perkiomen point traditions, with documented sites across various regions of Virginia.

name: Savannah River tradition
description: Late Archaic projectile point tradition extremely prevalent along James River headwaters and well documented across Virginia's mountainous regions, showing size reduction over time.

name: Perkiomen points
description: Late Archaic projectile point type found clustered in northern Shenandoah Valley and known for southern Ridge and Valley, reported rarely in far Southwestern Virginia.

name: Susquehanna points
description: Projectile point type reported rarely across far Southwestern Virginia during Late Archaic period.

name: Ledbetter points
description: Projectile point type typical of Tennessee Valley Archaic tradition found in Southwest Virginia.

name: Otarre Stemmed
description: Stemmed projectile point type typical of Tennessee Valley Archaic tradition found in Southwest Virginia.

name: Daugherty's Cave
description: Archaeological site where reduction in Savannah River point size over time has been documented.

name: Edd's Mill
description: Archaeological site where numerous small FCR filled pits were recorded in probable Late Archaic context.

name: Late Archaic Period
description: Archaeological period traditionally ending around 1000 BC, characterized by point forms such as Small Savannah River.

name: Early Woodland Period
description: Archaeological period traditionally beginning around 1000 BC, marked by the adoption of ceramic technology.

name: Small Savannah River
description: Point form traditionally thought to date to the later portion of the Late Archaic period.

name: Savannah River-like point forms
description: Point forms that occur with early ceramics, demonstrating the transition between Late Archaic and Early Woodland periods.

name: Middle Atlantic
description: Geographic region where recent excavations have pushed back the date of ceramic technology adoption past 1000 BC.

name: Southeast
description: Geographic region where Late Archaic ceramic associations are well-known.

name: Chesapeake Bay
description: A major estuary and bay system along Virginia's coast where Late Archaic occupations are commonly found.

name: Piedmont
description: A physiographic region in Virginia characterized by uplands and intervening drainages where Late Archaic occupations occurred.

name: Allegheny highlands

description: A wetland area in Virginia where Late Archaic settlement concentrations, particularly Savannah River components, are found.

name: Ridge and Valley
description: A physiographic region in western Virginia where Late Archaic settlement concentrations occur.

name: Coastal Plain
description: A physiographic region in eastern Virginia associated with expanding estuarine settings during the Late Archaic period.

description: A cultural and chronological period characterized by settlement patterns tied to environmental shifts, intensifying subsistence regimes, and technological developments.

name: Arnold Valley
description: A valley nestled between the west slope of the Blue Ridge and the James River, where Late Archaic was the most common component identified (39.7% of known total).

name: James River
description: A major river in Virginia with western headwater branches showing a preponderance of Savannah River components, located near Arnold Valley where it breaks through to the Piedmont.

name: Piedmont
description: Geographic region where the James River breaks through from the Blue Ridge area.

name: Ridge and Valley province
description: Geographic province where settlement patterns show a Middle Archaic to Late Archaic continuum similar to the Coastal Plain, with increased Late Archaic settlement along major drainages.

name: Coastal Plain
description: Geographic region that shows a Middle Archaic to Late Archaic settlement continuum similar to that found west of the Blue Ridge.

name: Middle Archaic Period
description: Archaeological period that shows a settlement continuum with the Late Archaic in both the Coastal Plain and west of the Blue Ridge.

name: Early Woodland Period
description: Archaeological period during which settlement along major drainages intensified significantly in the Ridge and Valley province.

name: Northern Blue Ridge
description: High elevation region of western Virginia where Late Archaic components, particularly Savannah River, are prevalent.

name: Southern Blue Ridge
description: High elevation region of western Virginia where Late Archaic components, particularly Savannah River, are prevalent.

name: Horse Heaven Road Site
description: A high elevation archaeological site in Wythe County where extensive quarrying and reduction of quartzite for Savannah River production was noted, along with generalized subsistence-oriented activities.

name: Wythe County
description: A county in Virginia containing the Horse Heaven Road Site where quartzite quarrying and Savannah River production occurred.

name: Far Southwest Virginia
description: Geographic region of Virginia that has yielded evidence for intensive Late Archaic settlement essentially unmatched in other regions of the Commonwealth.

name: Indian Creek
description: A substantial intermediate order tributary to the Powell River in Lee County, with associated sites excavated along Route 58 that yielded evidence for intensive Late Archaic settlement.

name: Powell River
description: River in Lee County along which numerous Late Archaic sites have been excavated, containing evidence for extensive and prolonged settlement.

name: Lee County
description: County in Virginia where numerous Late Archaic sites have been excavated along the Indian Creek/Powell River environs.

name: Graham Site
description: Archaeological site located along the Powell River that contained intact hearth and midden features, and possible evidence of steatite bowl manufacture.

name: 44LE165
description: Archaeological site where a cluster of post molds and a hearth feature was identified, with one post feature yielding a radiocarbon date of 4390+/- 40 BP.

name: Chadwell Site
description: Archaeological site that contained a human burial radiocarbon dated 4860+/-40 BP and a number of pit features associated with a substantial Late Archaic occupation.

name: 656 Elk Garden Site
description: Archaeological site where three shallow pit features yielded Late Archaic radiocarbon dates.

name: Edd's Mill Site
description: Archaeological site located along a minor Powell River tributary where a very large Late Archaic post feature was uncovered, yielding a steatite vessel fragment and carbonized hickory nut shells dated 3570+/- 70 BP.

name: Route 58
description: Route adjacent to Indian Creek along which a suite of Late Archaic sites was excavated.

name: Appalachian Plateau
description: Geographic feature that the Indian Creek locality fronts as a broad limestone valley.

name: Pennington Gap
description: Location where extensive survey and test excavation for the federal penitentiary demonstrated that Late Archaic settlement was prevalent across broad limestone uplands.

name: Late Archaic
description: Cultural period characterized by intensive settlement in far southwestern Virginia, with evidence including architectural remains, pit features, and human burials pointing to long term occupations.

name: Middle Archaic
description: Cultural period that co-occurs with Late Archaic components elsewhere in Virginia but was not noted on the Indian Creek Valley sites.

name: Hypsithermal
description: Climatic period preceding the Late Archaic, after which increased moisture and climatic amelioration made limestone bottoms and sinkhole locations attractive for Late Archaic settlement.

name: steatite bowl
description: Artifact type with possible evidence of manufacture found at the Graham Site and fragment found at Edd's Mill Site.

name: Commonwealth
description: Reference to Virginia, used in context of comparing Late Archaic settlement intensity across regions.

name: Late Archaic
description: Archaeological period characterized by occupations across Virginia with similar functions, typically interpreted as short term encampments or seasonal base camp settlements.

name: Commonwealth
description: Reference to Virginia as a whole, mentioned in context of archaeological sites across the state outside the Southwest region.

name: Southwest Virginia
description: Geographic region of Virginia distinguished by the presence of architectural remains and favorable preservation characteristics of limestone derived soils.

name: steatite vessels
description: Archaeological artifacts whose presence supports interpretation of sites as seasonal base camp settlements.

name: Falls
description: Geographic location where fishing/processing stations may have been located during the Late Archaic period.

name: Late Archaic
description: A cultural period characterized by intensification of subsistence practices, technological innovation, and development of trade networks, marked by the use of steatite vessels and specialized lithic procurement.

name: Savannah River
description: A type of hafted biface tool produced from quartzite and other tough materials during the Late Archaic period.

name: steatite vessels
description: Durable cooking vessels made from steatite (soapstone) that represented a novel technology unique to the Late Archaic period, used for various cooking activities including oil rendering and food processing.

name: quartzite
description: A tough material utilized during the Late Archaic for the production of Savannah River hafted biface forms.

name: Poverty Point
description: An archaeological site or cultural complex where steatite vessels had prominence, suggesting their use as prestige items.

name: Eastern North America
description: Geographic region where Truncer studied the age and occurrence of steatite across various settings.

name: Southeast
description: Geographic region mentioned in context of previously postulated models for steatite vessel manufacture and use spread.

name: Northeast
description: Geographic region mentioned in context of previously postulated models for steatite vessel manufacture and use spread.

name: Hunter's Home site
description: Archaeological site in New York where Hart et al. analyzed microbotanical remains from steatite vessels to test mast processing hypothesis.

name: New York
description: State where the Hunter's Home site is located.

name: Virginia
description: State where steatite vessel fragments occur in various settings and site types, mirroring broader distributional trends.

name: Eastern Seaboard
description: Geographic region throughout which steatite vessel technology spread.

name: steatite vessels
description: Stone vessels that may have been prestige items used for cooking various plant materials and animal flesh, with excellent thermal properties.

name: Late Archaic
description: A cultural period characterized by developing subsistence patterns with increasing emphasis on collective resource exploitation and organized harvesting activities.

name: Archaic times
description: An earlier cultural period preceding the Late Archaic, characterized by more diffuse settlement and subsistence ways.

name: Coastal Plain
description: A geographic region where anadromous fish were available only at limited locations during the Late Archaic period.

name: Broadspear points
description: Stone projectile points that were once thought to be specialized fishing implements but are now recognized as having diverse uses including projectile tips and cutting implements.

name: platform hearths
description: Archaeological features that were once thought to function for drying and smoking fish catches but are now recognized as having diverse cooking and processing functions.

name: fire-cracked rock features
description: Archaeological features interpreted as associated with drying, smoking fish, indirect heating activities, stone boiling, or use in earth ovens.

name: earth ovens
description: Cooking features that may have been associated with fire-cracked rock deposits through indirect heating activities.

name: Dent (1995)
description: A researcher who pointed out that collective harvesting and processing of anadromous fish would have required coordination of effort and organization of labor.

name: Blue Ridge
description: Mountain region where chestnut groves became well established by 3500 BP, with east-facing slopes and foothills important for mast production.

name: Nash
description: Scholar who provided palynological evidence in 2009 about chestnut grove establishment in the northern Blue Ridge by 3500 BP.

name: Holland
description: Scholar who suggested in 1978 that east-facing Blue Ridge slopes were important draws for central Virginia groups due to rich mast-producing forest communities.

name: Piedmont
description: Geographic region with uplands that typically have less rich mast-producing forest communities compared to Blue Ridge foothills.

name: Late Archaic
description: Cultural period characterized by production of minor food surpluses and abundant carbonized hickory shell remains in Virginia archaeological contexts.

name: Virginia
description: Geographic region where Late Archaic archaeological contexts yield abundant carbonized hickory shell remains and where direct evidence for storage is largely absent.

name: Magnolia Site
description: Archaeological site located near the Dismal Swamp with a large storage pit approximately 2.5 meters in dimension dating to 4150+/-50 BP.

name: Dismal Swamp
description: Geographic location near which the Magnolia Site is situated.

name: Blanton
description: Scholar who reported in 2002 on a large storage pit at the Magnolia Site that yielded carbonized hickory shell and suggested it was used for short-term storage of nut crops.

name: Southwest Virginia
description: Geographic region where soil characteristics are favorable for preservation but Late Archaic features consist mainly of post holes and small shallow pits used for cooking rather than storage.

name: Late Archaic
description: A cultural period characterized by purposeful environmental modification, long-distance trade networks, and nascent horticultural practices. Features include the use of steatite vessels and evidence of plant domestication activities.

name: Eastern North America
description: A broad geographical region considered by Smith as a locus for independent plant domestication, where seasonal flooding along rivers provided ground disturbance that supported pioneering plant species exploitation.

name: Virginia
description: A region where additional site excavations are recommended for future research into Late Archaic trade and exchange networks.

name: steatite vessels
description: Stone vessels that were transported long distances from their quarry sources during the Late Archaic period, likely utilizing boat transport, and serving as evidence of extensive trade networks.

name: Chenopodium sp.
description: An indigenous seed-bearing plant species that was intensively exploited and developed into nascent horticultural practices during the Late Archaic period.

name: Iva annua
description: An indigenous seed-bearing plant species that was intensively exploited and developed into nascent horticultural practices during the Late Archaic period.

name: groundstone artifacts
description: Stone artifacts that moved significant distances from their source locations during the Late Archaic period as part of exchange networks.

name: Dismal Swamp
description: A highly productive ecological setting recognized as a hot spot for Late Archaic settlement and trade in southeastern Virginia, offering exceptionally rich resources to Late Archaic peoples.

name: Late Archaic Period
description: A chronological period characterized by intensive settlement and trade activities in the Dismal Swamp area and throughout Virginia.

name: Nansemond River
description: A river adjacent to the Dismal Swamp that likely encompassed the highly productive ecological setting exploited by Late Archaic peoples.

name: Elizabeth River
description: A river near the Dismal Swamp that was part of the rich ecological setting exploited by Late Archaic peoples.

name: Lower James River
description: An estuarine area that may have been part of the highly productive ecological setting near the Dismal Swamp.

name: Virginia
description: The geographic region where Late Archaic settlement patterns and trade networks developed, including the Dismal Swamp area.

name: Piedmont
description: A geographic region that provided steatite and stone tool materials accessed through trade networks with Dismal Swamp groups.

name: steatite
description: A stone material obtained from Piedmont resources through trade, used for cooking by Late Archaic groups.

name: ground stone tools
description: Stone implements obtained through trade with Piedmont groups, used for boat manufacture and other heavy work by Dismal Swamp peoples.

name: banded bannerstones
description: Exotic trade items that originated at least 650 km away from southeastern Virginia, likely obtained for status rather than functional purposes.

name: semi-lunar knives
description: Rare artifacts found in the Middle Atlantic region with apparent affiliation to Northeast cultures, possibly associated with women's work and food processing.

name: Middle Atlantic
description: A regional area where semi-lunar knives are rare finds, encompassing the southeastern Virginia area.

name: Northeast
description: A cultural region with which semi-lunar knives have affiliation, possibly the source of dislocated groups that moved south.

name: Inuit
description: An Arctic cultural group among whom ulus (semi-lunar knives) are associated with women's work such as processing fish and skins.

name: Native North America
description: The broader geographic and cultural region where non-game food processing was typically performed by women.

name: Late Archaic
description: A cultural period representing a critical juncture when hunting and gathering societies began transitioning from egalitarian order to incipient social ranking.

name: Virginia
description: The geographic region where Perkiomen points occur in geographic isolation, providing evidence for in-migration of distinct cultural groups.

name: Perkiomen points
description: Projectile points found in Virginia in geographic isolation, regarded as evidence for in-migration of distinct cultural groups from core areas to the north.

name: Savannah River material culture
description: Material culture associated with groups that had a focused orientation toward estuarine/riverine resources and spread through adaptive radiation.

name: Savannah River groups
description: Cultural groups with a riverine orientation that coexisted with indigenous late Middle Archaic groups according to the adaptive radiation model.

name: Middle Archaic groups
description: Indigenous cultural groups that retained a more sylvan focus and coexisted with the riverine-oriented Savannah River groups.

name: Mouer et al.
description: Researchers who elaborated on the traditional migration theme with an adaptive radiation model for Late Archaic cultural change in 1981.

name: Catlin et al.
description: Researchers who in 1982 responded with a model for Late Archaic cultural development stressing indigenous population growth, environmental change, and trade over migration.

name: Savannah River
description: A Late Archaic cultural tradition characterized by specific material culture traits, with radiocarbon dates suggesting temporal progression from coastal areas westward.

name: Mouer's adaptive radiation model
description: A model proposing that 'daughter groups' moved west along Virginia's major river valleys, taking Savannah River material culture and life ways inland with them as a slower, ongoing process.

name: Virginia
description: The state where archaeological research is being conducted, with major river valleys serving as migration routes for cultural groups.

name: Coastal Plain
description: An eastern geographic zone in Virginia where early Savannah River dates have been obtained, characterized by uneven distribution of resources.

name: Fall Line
description: A geographic zone between Coastal Plain and Piedmont, strategically important for harvesting anadromous fish and showing early Savannah River dates.

name: Outer Piedmont
description: Part of the eastern zone in Virginia where Savannah River dates have been obtained.

name: Blue Ridge
description: A mountain range serving as a geographic boundary, with areas west of it forming a distinct zone for archaeological analysis.

name: Piedmont
description: An expansive region in Virginia where acceptable Savannah River dates are lacking except near the Fall Line.

name: Richmond
description: A city near the Fall Line in the vicinity of which some Piedmont Savannah River dates have been obtained.

name: Late Archaic period
description: A cultural period characterized by population growth, intensified resource exploitation, and migration patterns that shaped settlement dynamics.

name: Dismal Swamp
description: A highly productive area of ecological diversity that would have been subject to territorial claims during the Late Archaic period.

name: fluted points
description: Early projectile points representing one end of the traditional archaeological progression from earliest to latest periods.

name: small triangles
description: Later projectile points representing the other end of the traditional archaeological progression from earliest to latest periods.

name: Sassaman
description: Scholar who critiques migration-based explanations for Late Archaic material culture patterning, arguing that large-scale movement was facilitated by existing social connections.

name: Late Archaic Period
description: Archaeological period characterized by large-scale movement of peoples and transmission of material culture traits including broad blade bifaces.

name: Pagoulatos
description: Scholar critical of migration as a driver of Late Archaic cultural change, arguing for diffusion-based processes in material culture transmission.

name: Middle Atlantic
description: Geographic region where Pagoulatos conducted archaeological data collection to test migration-based theories.

name: Northeast
description: Geographic region where Pagoulatos conducted archaeological data collection to test migration-based theories.

name: broad blade bifaces
description: Late Archaic material culture traits that Pagoulatos argues were transmitted primarily through diffusion-based processes rather than migration.

name: Coastal Plain
description: A geographic region in Virginia where Savannah River radiocarbon dates have been collected, representing one of the areas analyzed in the archaeological study.
source_section: The-Archaeology-of-Virginias-First-Peoples/Table 6.4: Virginia Savannah River RadiocarbonDates by Region

name: Fall Line
description: A geographic region in Virginia where Savannah River radiocarbon dates have been collected, representing the boundary between the Coastal Plain and Piedmont regions.
source_section: The-Archaeology-of-Virginias-First-Peoples/Table 6.4: Virginia Savannah River RadiocarbonDates by Region

name: Outer Piedmont
description: A geographic region in Virginia where Savannah River radiocarbon dates have been collected, part of the broader Piedmont physiographic province.
source_section: The-Archaeology-of-Virginias-First-Peoples/Table 6.4: Virginia Savannah River RadiocarbonDates by Region

name: Savannah River
description: An artifact type or cultural tradition represented by radiocarbon dates from Virginia archaeological sites, dating to approximately 4150 RCYBP.
source_section: The-Archaeology-of-Virginias-First-Peoples/Table 6.4: Virginia Savannah River RadiocarbonDates by Region

name: Late Archaic
description: A cultural and chronological period characterized by diverse lifeways, territorial hunter-gatherers, and complex intra-group and inter-group dynamics. Associated with radiocarbon dates around 3600-3920 BP.

name: Piedmont
description: A geographic region mentioned as containing expansive and productive floodplains that may have been subject to Late Archaic territorial claims.

name: Big Meadows
description: An archaeological site located straddling the Blue Ridge with evidence of Late Archaic occupation, including charcoal lenses, steatite vessel fragments, and evidence of possible forest manipulation. Radiocarbon dates average 3600 B.P.

name: Blue Ridge
description: A mountain range that Big Meadows straddles, serving as a geographic boundary between the Piedmont and Great Valley regions.

name: Great Valley
description: A geographic region mentioned as being inhabited by culturally linked groups during the Late Archaic period, separated from the Piedmont by the Blue Ridge.

name: steatite vessel fragments
description: Stone cooking vessels recovered at Big Meadows, interpreted as markers of interaction and regional exchange, possibly linked to socially charged feasting during seasonal abundance.

name: Nash
description: A researcher who studied Big Meadows and proposed theories about Late Archaic territoriality over chestnut groves and their active maintenance by hunter-gatherers.

name: Pagoulatos
description: An author cited in 2010 work regarding ideas and cultural concepts related to Late Archaic diversity.

name: Late Archaic Period
description: Archaeological period characterized by developing socio-political complexity and communal labor organization, with evidence of complex mortuary behaviors and territorial connections.

name: Perkiomen points
description: Projectile points found in southeastern Virginia that appear to have been purposely broken or heat shattered before being placed in the ground as part of mortuary practices.

name: southeastern Virginia
description: Geographic region where Perkiomen points have been found in apparent mortuary contexts.

name: Virginia
description: State where researchers have limited archaeological evidence for Late Archaic social complexity and mortuary behavior compared to the Northeast.

name: Northeast
description: Geographic region where mortuary complexity in the form of elaborate burial treatments and funerary objects is documented for the Late Archaic period.

name: Dismal Swamp
description: Location where exotic items have been recovered from surface contexts, with potential ceremonial associations and implications for group identity and territorial connection.

name: Woodland period
description: Archaeological period during which societies likely used exotic items as expressions of individual status.

name: Commonwealth of Virginia
description: The state of Virginia, referenced as containing the full range of physical settings where Late Archaic site occupations are found.

name: Late Archaic Period
description: A cultural and chronological period characterized by diverse site occupations, settlement patterns, and varying degrees of social organization and complexity across Virginia.

name: Savannah River
description: A type of artifact manufacture or tool tradition shared by Late Archaic groups, mentioned alongside steatite vessel use as a material culture attribute.

name: steatite vessel
description: A type of stone vessel used by Late Archaic groups, mentioned as a shared material culture attribute alongside Savannah River manufacture.

name: Coastal Plain
description: A physiographic region used as an example of the Late Archaic cultural landscape characterized by multiple territories occupied by different groups.

name: Blanton
description: An archaeologist or researcher who in 2003 proposed that the Late Archaic landscape of the Coastal Plain was characterized by a mosaic of multiple territories occupied by different groups.

name: Late Archaic Period
description: Archaeological period characterized by diagnostic projectile points and steatite vessels, with significant cultural developments in Virginia.

description: An expansive region of Virginia south of the James River between the Blue Ridge and the Fall Line, roughly the size of New Jersey, where site data is essentially lacking.

description: A geographic feature that serves as one boundary of the southern Piedmont region in Virginia.

name: Roanoke River system
